description_long: This object is a large, mounted rotary-barrel weapon resembling a Gatling or minigun, with a robust, cylindrical main body and six parallel barrels extending from the front. The body is heavily armored with visible paneling, bolts, and reinforced sections, suggesting durability and high firepower. The weapon is mounted on a solid, angular base, likely for stability during operation, and features a prominent handle or carrying bar on the top rear. The barrels are encased in a rotating shroud, and the rear section includes a boxy component that may house ammunition or a power source. The surface is metallic with a weathered, industrial finish, showing signs of wear and use. This weapon is designed for fixed emplacement, likely for defensive purposes in urban environments such as checkpoints or fortified positions.
description: A heavy, mounted rotary-barrel weapon with a rugged, metallic body and six barrels, set on a reinforced base for stability. The design is industrial and robust, suitable for defensive urban deployment.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the six-barrel cluster, the main armored body, and the angular mounting base.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular arrangement of the barrels and the central bore, with the mounting base visible below.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the rear boxy component, handle, and the full length of the barrels from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the back of the weapon, showing the handle, rear housing, and the mounting base.
